report_name: UK Civil Service Pension Scheme Suffers Data Breach Under Capita's Troubled Administration

vulnerabilities.name: Insecure Direct Object Reference in CSPS Portal
vulnerabilities.description: A technical fault in the UK Civil Service Pension Scheme (CSPS) online portal's authorization logic allowed 138 members to view or download the Annual Benefit Statements of other individuals for a 35-minute period. This indicates a flaw similar to an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR).

identities.name: UK Civil Service Pension Scheme (CSPS)
identities.description: A UK government pension scheme whose member data was exposed in a data breach due to a technical fault on its online portal.

identities.name: Capita
identities.description: The outsourcing contractor responsible for administering the UK Civil Service Pension Scheme. A technical glitch on the portal they manage led to a data breach.

identities.name: Information Commissioner's Office (ICO)
identities.description: The UK's data protection regulator to which the data breach was reported.

identities.name: UK Cabinet Office
identities.description: The UK government department that oversees the CSPS contract and described the data breach as 'unacceptable'.

indicators.name: CSPS Portal Sensitive Benefit Statement Access
indicators.pattern:
title: CSPS Portal Sensitive Benefit Statement Access
id: 7c5a8d1e-9f0b-4c6a-8d2e-1b9f0a8d2e1b
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects access to the Annual Benefit Statement API endpoint on the UK Civil Service Pension Scheme portal. 
  A technical flaw in this endpoint led to a data breach where users could access other users' statements. 
  Monitoring access can help identify anomalous patterns, although legitimate access will also trigger this rule.
references:
  - https://www.pensionsage.com/pa/PAC-correspondence-on-CSPS-transition-reveals-unacceptable-data-breach.php
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-15
modified: 2026-04-15
tags:
  - attack.t1213
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate user access to their own statements.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains|all:
      - '/api/v1/user/'
      - '/statement'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects access to the API endpoint for Annual Benefit Statements which was the source of the data breach. While legitimate traffic will match, it allows for monitoring of a known sensitive endpoint.

relationships.source_name: CSPS Portal Sensitive Benefit Statement Access
relationships.target_name: Insecure Direct Object Reference in CSPS Portal
